title: The biggest challenge of AI is not people or technology
author: CD Athuraliya
excerpt: Yes, that’s correct. Well, I think so and let me explain. I think the biggest challenge of AI right now is making it an industry or an engineering field. All the other challenges were there for a while if you really think about it. There can be slow downs and booms in fundamental research, talent can be in short supply, we still make progress. But I think we are yet to define AI as an industry. And we need to do it right away!

title: AI can solve all your business problems. Or maybe not!
author: Janak Gunatilleke
excerpt: A 2017 survey of more than 3,000 executives by MIT Sloan Management Review and Boston Consulting Group revealed that almost 85% believed that Artificial Intelligence (AI) will give their companies or help them maintain a competitive advantage. Non-public AI companies raised $15.2B funding in 2017, an increase of 141% compared to 2016.

title: Musings after Amsterdam (and World Summit AI 2018)
author: Janak Gunatilleke
excerpt: A couple of weeks ago I attended the World Summit AI 2018 in Amsterdam. Coming from a non-technology background (I originally trained as a medical doctor) and as someone who had since worked as a management consultant and more recently have been working with various startups (including ConscientAI), I was keen to understand more about AI from a business point of view.

title: 5 things to consider to make your AI project a success
author: Janak Gunatilleke
excerpt: According to Gartner analysts, up to 85% of big data projects fail. I suspect that for AI projects, especially if you count the ones that never start, the failure rate is even higher. What are the reasons for the high failure rate with AI projects? Drawing on my technology consulting experience from working at Accenture UK and wider transformation project experience within the highly complex environment of the UK National Health Service (NHS), I wanted to identify the causes and potential solutions.

title: Online prediction and model pipelining for Google BERT
author: CD Athuraliya
excerpt: This is going to be a short one. While others argue over GPT-2, we had to dig a little deeper into Google BERT. If you are a follower of deep learning research, especially on NLP side, BERT needs no introduction at this point in time. Since its initial release last October, dozens more resources have been added highlighting the significance of this work by Google. The official blog post itself is a very good resource to understand the basics.

title: 5 takeaways from AI Everything Summit for Sri Lanka
author: Janak Gunatilleke
excerpt: Keen to explore the latest developments in AI and to learn more about how it was being used in the region, I attended the inaugural AI Everything summit in Dubai. Having also recently been thinking about the current state of AI in Sri Lanka and what it could be (or should be) in the future, I found that the summit provided some very useful perspectives.

title: Showcasing collaborative research at the BCS AI & Data Analytics Summit 2026
excerpt: We were delighted to be part of the BCS AI & Data Analytics Summit 2026, organized by the Sri Lanka Section of BCS, The Chartered Institute for IT. Held in Colombo, the summit brought together policymakers, researchers, industry leaders and technology professionals to discuss the future of AI and data analytics in Sri Lanka, with a strong focus on collaboration, innovation and real-world impact.
